all.critique: The logic calls model.forward(x) directly instead of using the module call (e.g., model(x)), which may bypass important PyTorch features (such as hooks and proper handling of certain layers) and could lead to unexpected behavior.

The design heavily relies on specific configuration keys (e.g., config.training.cv, config.training.nlp, config.training.graph) without clearly defined fallbacks. This dependency could cause runtime issues if the configuration file is incomplete or not strictly adhered to.

Although validation and test data loaders are provided, their integration into the training loop is only mentioned as optional. This lack of explicit validation steps may hinder proper monitoring and early-stopping mechanisms as outlined in the experimental design.

The curriculum scheduler is assumed to return a scalar weight for loss reweighting. This simplistic approach might not fully capture the more complex data selection or reweighting strategies described in the paper, potentially deviating from the intended curriculum learning methodology.
